architecture_summary: Add GitHubCheckRunWriter as a pure receipt planner that builds the GitHub Checks REST endpoint and request payload, hashes the payload, classifies plan_only, dry_run, and write_approved modes, and emits GitHubCheckRunWriteReceipt without performing a GitHub API call. Plan-only and dry-run receipts cannot bind response evidence. Write-approved receipts only admit the external write when approval, installation id, external execution receipt, response check-run id, and response payload hash are present. Secret-shaped material blocks admission before any check-run write claim can be recorded. The receipt schema is indexed in the governance protocol manifest so public schema validation can witness the boundary.
data_model_changes: add GitHubCheckRunWriteRequest for repository, head SHA, check payload, mode, evidence refs, approval refs, external execution refs, response refs, and metadata
add GitHubCheckRunWriteReceipt for endpoint, request payload, payload hash, blocked reasons, required controls, external write admission, and non-live execution flags
add schema-backed receipt metadata proving terminal closure is separate, GitHub API was not called, authentication was not performed, payload hash is bound, and secret absence was verified
rollback_plan: Remove GitHubCheckRunWriter, its receipt schema, protocol manifest entry, protocol document count update, focused tests, proof coverage matrix surface and generated fixture/doc changes, and SDLC evidence artifacts, then rerun schema validation, protocol manifest validation, public repository surface validation, proof coverage matrix validation, focused check-run writer tests, SDLC artifact validation, security review validation, release readiness validation, and workspace governance preflight.
migration_plan: No persistence, credential, deployment, GitHub App, branch protection, or runtime route migration is required. The change adds a local deterministic planner and schema only.
